name: Temperature — Reading the Thermometer
whatIsIt: TEMPERATURE tells us how hot or cold something is. We measure it with a THERMOMETER, and the number is read in DEGREES CELSIUS (°C). A higher number means hotter; a lower number means colder. When it is very cold, temperature can even go BELOW zero, like -3°C on a winter morning in the hills.
whyNeeded: We check temperature every day — the weather report, a fever thermometer, the oven, the fridge, and boiling water for tea. Reading a scale correctly and knowing hotter means a bigger number keeps us safe and healthy.
realLifeUses: Checking if you have a fever (normal body is about 37°C)
Reading the weather — 42°C in a Delhi summer is very hot
Setting the fridge cold and the oven hot
Knowing water boils at 100°C and ice melts at 0°C

vocabulary.meaning: How hot or cold something is.
The unit we use to write temperature.

story.title: Anaya's Fever Check
story.text: Anaya felt warm, so Amma put a thermometer under her arm. 'It shows 39°C,' said Amma. 'Normal is about 37°C, so 39 is a little fever.' Later they saw the news: the hill station was only 4°C, but their city was 38°C. 'The bigger number is hotter,' Anaya smiled, 'so our city is much warmer than the hills today.'
story.extractedProblem: Anaya's thermometer reads 39°C and normal is 37°C. Is that a fever, and by how many degrees is it above normal?
story.answerInStory: Yes, a small fever — 2°C above normal (39 - 37 = 2).

visual.caption: Think of a thermometer as an upright number line: 0°C at freezing, 100°C at boiling. The higher the mark, the hotter.

teachingGallery.title: Hotter means higher
teachingGallery.note: As the reading goes up, it gets hotter.

standardMethod.summary: Find the mark the thermometer points to, read the number, and add the unit °C. To compare, remember the bigger number is hotter.
steps: Look at where the liquid or pointer stops on the thermometer.
Read the nearest number on the scale.
Write it with the unit, like 30°C.
To compare two temperatures, the larger number is hotter; below 0 (like -2°C) is colder than 0°C.

workedExamples.problem: A thermometer reads 30°C in the morning and 38°C at noon. How much hotter is noon?
Ice melts at 0°C. A freezer is at -6°C. Is the freezer warmer or colder than melting ice?
steps: So the freezer is colder than 0°C.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinking a bigger number means colder.
commonMistakes.fix: A BIGGER temperature number means HOTTER. 40°C is hotter than 20°C.
Always add the unit: temperature is written in degrees Celsius, like 25°C.

commonMistakes.mistake: Choosing a length tool (like a ruler) to measure temperature.
commonMistakes.fix: Temperature is measured with a THERMOMETER; a ruler measures length.

commonMistakes.mistake: Swapping the boiling and freezing points of water.
commonMistakes.fix: Water FREEZES/melts at 0°C and BOILS at 100°C — the high number is boiling.

medium.q: Morning 22°C, afternoon 31°C. How many degrees warmer is the afternoon?

challenge.q: A fever is 40°C and normal is 37°C. How many degrees above normal?

questions.q: A bigger temperature number means it is…
Noon 36°C, evening 29°C. How many degrees did it drop?
We write temperature with the unit…

teachBackPrompt: Look at a weather app or the news with a grown-up. Read today's temperature aloud with the unit (like '34°C'), and say whether it is hotter or colder than yesterday and by how many degrees.

revisionCard.summary: Temperature = how hot or cold, measured by a thermometer in degrees Celsius (°C). Bigger number = hotter. 0°C = ice melts, 37°C = body, 100°C = water boils. Below 0 (like -3°C) is colder than 0.

rememberIt.hook: Higher number = hotter; 0 freeze, 37 body, 100 boil
rememberIt.unpack: Read the mark, add °C. Below zero is colder than zero.

studentQuestions.a: °C means degrees Celsius — the unit India and most countries use for temperature.

studentQuestions.q: Can temperature be less than zero?
studentQuestions.a: Yes! On cold mountains it can be -2°C or lower — colder than freezing.

funFacts: Normal human body temperature is about 37°C — that's why a thermometer reading much above it means a fever.
